headline_claim.note: LogisticRegression proxy on the same features/split; the MLP probe's reported headline is acc=0.95 / auc=0.98 (metrics.json).

1_disjointness.verdict: PASS -- splits are disjoint at both prompt and index level

2_label_shuffle.verdict: PASS -- true=0.925 high, shuffled=0.425 ~chance; no features->label leakage path

3_text_confounds.interpretation: Trivial text baselines stay well below the probe -- the probe's score is not explained by surface text features alone.
3_text_confounds.verdict: OK -- text-only baselines (tfidf 0.575) trail the probe

4_scaler_discipline.reference: train_probe.py step 4: Scaler.fit(X[tr]) then transform train/val/test with that train-fit scaler
4_scaler_discipline.verdict: PASS -- standardization is fit on the train split only; no test statistics leak into the scaler.

overall_verdict: LEGITIMATE -- no leakage and no dominant text artifact; the headline reflects real activation-level signal.
